description_long: This house features a single-story extension with a sloped shed roof covered in dark gray shingles and two large skylights. The front facade is dominated by expansive floor-to-ceiling windows and a central double glass door framed in light wood, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The structure is attached to a brick wall, which also includes a single rectangular window above the extension. The side elevations reveal clerestory windows beneath the roofline, enhancing the modern aesthetic and providing additional daylight. The rear view is a plain brick wall, indicating the extension is likely an addition to an existing building. The overall design is contemporary, with clean lines, large glass surfaces, and a focus on openness and transparency.
description: A modern single-story house extension with a sloped roof, large skylights, and expansive glass windows framed in light wood, attached to a brick wall.
description_view_0: Front view showing large glass windows, a central double glass door, and two skylights on the sloped roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped roof, clerestory windows, and the connection to the brick wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain brick wall with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the sloped roof, clerestory windows, and the upper window on the brick wall.
attribute_window_arrangement: large picture windows with clerestory and skylights
attribute_entryway_design: Central double glass door framed in light wood, flush with the window wall, no steps or porch
